results.reply: For EU fiat pay-ins, pricing is usage-based with no monthly minimums. SEPA Instant pay-ins start from 0.15% + €0.20 per transaction, and at your volume (€20M+/month equivalent) we usually move to custom enterprise pricing, often below 15bps blended depending on the rails and flow. If helpful, I can share a more precise view on a short 30-minute discovery call: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Totally fair question. If you’re already set up with BVNK, the reason teams look at Ivy is usually whether they want a more crypto-native setup for pay-ins and pay-outs across rails like SEPA Instant, ACH, FPS, and cards. If it’s helpful, I can share where we tend to fit best and we can do a quick 30-min comparison call: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Thanks for checking. I can’t confirm GBP Open Banking from the materials I have in front of me, so I don’t want to overstate anything. If helpful, I can verify this with our team and come back to you, or you can grab a slot here and we can cover it on a quick call: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Thanks, that’s helpful. A VASP licence in India plus demand for EUR/GBP, including MENA, sounds like a good basis for the conversation. Looking forward to the call — happy to dig into the compliance setup and where Ivy could fit on the fiat/stablecoin flow then.

Thanks for clarifying — I understand the setup and why Mollie may not fit. I can’t confirm from my side whether we can support open banking payment initiation / real-time confirmation for a non-licensed marketplace in that exact model without checking with our team first. If helpful, I can have the right colleague review this and we can go through your setup on a short discovery call here: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Thanks for the detailed context — that’s very helpful. Based on what you described, I don’t want to overstate anything on payment confirmation/initiation, the licensing angle, or whether Mollie is required, as I’d need to confirm that internally rather than guess. Best next step would be a short 30-min discovery call so we can go through your setup properly and I can bring in the right person on our side if helpful: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Great to meet you too, and sounds good — looking forward to speaking. In case it’s helpful, here’s the calendar link again: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Talk soon.

Thanks for clarifying — that makes sense. I can’t confirm X chain support in our existing exchange-facing ramp/stablecoin flows from the information I have here, but it definitely sounds worth a brief discussion so we can understand the use case and check feasibility with the team. If helpful, you can grab a 30-minute slot here: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Thanks — that’s helpful. A VASP license in India plus EUR/GBP demand, including from MENA, gives us a useful starting point for the call. Looking forward to speaking at X and digging into the compliance setup, target corridors, and how you’re thinking about the integration.
